children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Compliance with the requirements imposed under this subchapter with respect to credit repair organizations shall be enforced under the Federal Trade Commission Act [ et seq.] by the Federal Trade Commission.15 U.S.C. 41

children.heading: Violations of this subchapter treated as violations of Federal Trade Commission Act
children.label: b Violations of this subchapter treated as violations of Federal Trade Commission Act

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For the purpose of the exercise by the Federal Trade Commission of the Commission’s functions and powers under the Federal Trade Commission Act [ et seq.], any violation of any requirement or prohibition imposed under this subchapter with respect to credit repair organizations shall constitute an unfair or deceptive act or practice in commerce in violation of section 5(a) of the Federal Trade Commission Act [].15 U.S.C. 4115 U.S.C. 45(a)

children.label: B
children.text: meets any other jurisdictional tests in the Federal Trade Commission Act.

children.label: A
children.text: may bring an action to enjoin such violation;

children.label: B
children.text: may bring an action on behalf of its residents to recover damages for which the person is liable to such residents under as a result of the violation; andsection 1679g of this title

children.label: C
children.text: in the case of any successful action under subparagraph (A) or (B), shall be awarded the costs of the action and reasonable attorney fees as determined by the court.

children.heading: Notice to Commission
children.label: A Notice to Commission
children.text: The State shall serve prior written notice of any civil action under paragraph (1) upon the Federal Trade Commission and provide the Commission with a copy of its complaint, except in any case where such prior notice is not feasible, in which case the State shall serve such notice immediately upon instituting such action.

children.label: i
children.text: to intervene in any action referred to in subparagraph (A);

children.label: ii
children.text: upon so intervening, to be heard on all matters arising in the action; and

children.heading: Investigatory powers
children.label: 3 Investigatory powers
children.text: For purposes of bringing any action under this subsection, nothing in this subsection shall prevent the chief law enforcement officer, or an official or agency designated by a State, from exercising the powers conferred on the chief law enforcement officer or such official by the laws of such State to conduct investigations or to administer oaths or affirmations or to compel the attendance of witnesses or the production of documentary and other evidence.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 4 Limitation
children.text: Whenever the Federal Trade Commission has instituted a civil action for violation of this subchapter, no State may, during the pendency of such action, bring an action under this section against any defendant named in the complaint of the Commission for any violation of this subchapter that is alleged in that complaint.
